stem: A industrial safety safety system uses 4 parallel sensors for redundancy. The hazard detected requires at least one sensor to function. sensor 1 has a reliability of 96%. sensor 2 has a reliability of 98%. sensor 3 has a reliability between 71% and 84% (uncertain due to limited operational data). sensor 4 has a reliability of 85%. Additionally, there is a common-cause failure probability (e.g., power outage, shared software bug) that could disable all sensors simultaneously, estimated between 3.4% and 6.3%. What is the overall probability that the hazard detected?

ground_truth.answer: P(hazard detected) ranges from 0.937 to 0.966. Point estimate: 0.951.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 4 sensors fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.04 × 0.02 × 0.29 × 0.15 = 0. P(works) = 1.0.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.04 × 0.02 × 0.16 × 0.15 = 0. P(works) = 1.0.
With common-cause failure (3.4%-6.3%): P(works) = (1-P_cc) × P(independent works). Range: [0.937, 0.966].
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
Ignoring common-cause failures that can disable all paths simultaneously
